text:
THE SPACE ECONOMY IS RIPPING

$RKLB + $ASTS

$COHR CEO says $NVDA $2B investment reflects a shift toward optical interconnects as AI clusters hit limits moving data over copper.

As GPU clusters scale to tens of thousands, the bottlenecks move beyond compute to optics, memory & networking.

$ONDS announced ~$6M in new orders for its Sentrycs counter-UAS systems from defense & homeland security customers in the Middle East.

The systems are designed to protect critical infrastructure & could serve as foundation for broader counter-drone programs.

$RKLB Mars Telecommunications Orbiter will provide the communications backbone for missions to Mars.

Enabling everything from scientific research to human exploration on the Red Planet.

Anduril expects to roughly double revenue this year to ~$4.3B & recently raised a $4B round at $60B valuation.

All of its systems run on Lattice OS which is a unified autonomy platform powering drones, submarines & surveillance systems that acts as an OS for modern warfare
Cathie Wood bought ~$15M of $HOOD today
$RKLB just completed its 83rd Electron launch & another win for its small launch program.

$AMD is proof the AI supercycle is big enough for two winners.

• 2020: $10B revenue, ~$1.4B operating profit
• 2025: $35B revenue, ~$3.7B operating income
• 2028E: $85B revenue, ~$28B operating income

OpenAI signed up for 1GW of Instinct GPUs starting H2 2026 with $META committing 6GW of AI infrastructure.

The market assumed $NVDA would take 90% while everyone else fought for scraps. In reality, the TAM is expanding so fast a credible second source can scale to tens of billions without taking share from Jensen.

Be Jeff Green:

• Sold $162M of $TTD at ~$120 in Jan 2025
• Stock later collapsed ~80%
• Just bought $148M at ~$25

Sold ~5x higher than he’s buying it back now.

$MRVL Q4 EARNINGS

• Revenue $2.22B vs Est. $2.21B
• EPS $0.80 vs Est. $0.79
• Net Income $685M vs Est. $681M
• Gross Margin: 59% vs. Est. 59%

Q1 Guidance
• Revenue $2.4B vs Est. $2.3B
• EPS $0.79 vs Est. $0.74
• Gross Margin: 59% vs. Est. 59%

Anthropic launched an “AI Exposure Index” tracking which white-collar jobs are most vulnerable to LLM automation.

Computer programmers rank as the most exposed with ~75% of tasks automatable though researchers say there’s little evidence AI has increased unemployment yet.

Does the future freak @CameronDawson out or is everything alright?

The CIO of @NewEdgeWealth joins Dan & @GuyAdami to break down AI trades, credit cracks, gold’s parabolic move, and why “data is dead” might not be as crazy as it sounds.

"You talk to anybody, they're like oh no, the consumer is fine. Never bet against the US consumer... I'm not making a bearish call. But I'm just saying, this is a time when we have to challenge dominant narrative narratives."

🎙️@CameronDawson w/ Dan & @GuyAdami

The national average of gasoline prices is now up 32 cents (13%) since last Friday (bottom panel).

If these prices hold the rest of the month, the gasoline rise alone will now add 0.4% to headline CPI. Add the Cleveland Fed's estimate that CORE CPI would rise 0.25%, and that March CPI could be 0.6% of 0.7%.

This could push March's Y/Y CPI to 3% (January was 2.4%), even higher for PCE.

The US 10-year yield is now up 20 bps since Friday's close. The biggest such rise in 11 months.

Why? See the retweet below.

Now gasoline prices are up 25 cents in three days (bottom panel).

If gas prices end the month at $3.25, this alone now adds 0.32% to March headline CPI.

And crude oil is soaring again today.
*US OIL TOPS $80 A BARREL FOR FIRST TIME SINCE JANUARY 2025

In today's Early Look: "Upside Down Days"

Yesterday, being long Oil $BNO) Wheat $WEAT), and Oil & Gas Stocks $XOP) was good for The Fam. The rest of my book was upside down. After a great run, it made me feel dumb

Yes. I have feelings too. 

That's exactly why I built a decision-making process to fade them and just execute. 

Is The Game hard? Just a wee bit harder than the post-pandemic home-gamer thought it was — back when everything the "Retail Investor" owned was 25-50% higher in October of last year.

“Let there be an entrance opened for me into realities; I have worn the fools cap too long.”
-Ralph Waldo Emerson

WTI crude just hit $82/barrel, its highest level since 2024 & up 16% in the last 5 days.

Polymarket is now pricing in a 75% chance of oil hitting $90 by the end of March, which would mark the highest level since 2023.

Bloomberg reports the U.S. is drafting a rule that would require licenses for nearly all AI accelerator exports worldwide, meaning $NVDA and $AMD shipments to almost any country would need U.S. approval.

The review would scale with order size, with smaller clusters like up to ~1,000 GB300s getting a lighter process, and massive builds above ~200,000 GPUs potentially requiring host-government involvement and added conditions.
$spy LOD. Markets didn’t like the potential of ground troops.

Godzilla didn't destroy Tokyo.

The carry trade might.

$20 trillion.

Zero rates.

20 years.

Global leverage nobody talks about. 

August 2024 was a preview. 

The $VIX hit 65.

I made this to explain why.

And the conclusion is critical.

Few.

I'm wrong all the time.

The only thing I'm sure of is conditions.

How much of the yen carry trade has unwound?

Speculative futures: ~85% cleared

Hedge fund forwards: ~20%

Japanese insurers/pensions: ~5%

FX swaps (¥2,281T gross): ~3%

Weighted avg: ~8-12% of total.

The visible layer got flushed. The $4T+ structural layer? 

Barely touched.

Costco just posted $4.58 EPS. Revenue up 9.1%. Digital sales up 22.6%.

The American consumer is still alive.

But for how long with oil at $81 and rising?

Costco thrives on volume. Volume dies when gas eats the paycheck.

It has begun.

You are about to understand this.

NOW PUT FUCKING NOTIFICATIONS ON FOR MY ACCOUNT.

Oil spikes.

Oil priced in Yen super spikes.

Japan panics.

They behind to sell Treasuries to defend the Yen (I believe happening now).

Stocks collapse as Yen rapidly appreciates on carry trade deleveraging.

Flight to safety trade BACK into Treasuries as stocks collapse.

The reverse carry trade, I believe, has begun.

Won’t delete if I’m wrong because I have balls.

Copper miners (COPX) down 6.65% on March 5.

Copper is the economy's EKG.

When Dr. Copper flatlines while oil spikes, the diagnosis is stagflation.

Not a theory. A reading.

The 10-year yield has risen four consecutive sessions to 4.14%.

Stocks down. Bonds down. Oil up. Gold down.

When everything sells off together, it's not a rotation.

It's a liquidity event.

VIX at 23.75. Up 12% in a single session.

Two weeks ago it was under 18.

Fear doesn't arrive gradually. It arrives all at once.

And we're not even past the first act.
